name: Garlic-Stuffed Mushrooms
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the Fresh Parmesan Cheese for this recipe.
2. Clean the mushrooms by removing the stems and reserving them.
3. Parboil the mushrooms in just enough water to cover, for 3 minutes, until barely cooked. Drain well, blot with paper towels, and set aside until needed.
4. If the stem tips are woody, trim away. Chop the stems coarsely.
5. Melt 2 tbsp butter in a skillet. Toss the chopped mushroom stems and scallions. Sauté until tender and most of the mushroom juices have evaporated. Add the pecans, bread crumbs, Parmesan cheese, and remaining tablespoon of butter. Stir until the butter is melted and absorbed. Stir in the Garlic Bechamel.
6. Season with salt and freshly ground pepper to taste.
7. Stuff each mushroom cap with the mixture, mounding it neatly over the top. Sprinkle each with some cayenne pepper.
8. Place stuffed mushrooms in a lightly oiled, shallow baking dish. Bake for 10 minutes or until heated through. Serve immediately.
Garlic Bechamel:
1. In a heavy saucepan, melt butter and whisk in flour. Cook the roux over low heat, stirring constantly for 3 to 4 minutes.
2. Whisk in scalded milk (about 2 1/2 cups). Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and cook gently, stirring frequently for 10 to 15 minutes.
3. Add salt and pepper, then whisk in garlic puree from 3 large heads of roasted garlic. Remove from the heat.
4. Beat an egg at room temperature in a bowl. Beat some of the sauce into the egg, then beat the egg mixture back into the garlic sauce. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.
5. Cover with plastic wrap directly on the surface of the sauce and store in the refrigerator until ready to use. Thin with milk before using if necessary.
